name: Italian Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine sugar, salt, yeast, and 2 cups flour.
2. In a 1-quart saucepan over low heat, heat butter or margarine and 1 ¾ cups water until very warm (120°F to 130°F). Do not melt the butter.
3. With mixer on low speed, gradually beat liquid into dry ingredients until just blended. Increase speed to medium; beat for 2 minutes, scraping bowl with a rubber spatula often.
4. Beat in an additional half cup flour to make a thick batter; continue beating at medium speed for another 2 minutes, scraping the bowl.
5. With a wooden spoon, stir in enough extra flour (about 1 ¾ cups) to form a soft dough. Knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes, adding more flour as needed.
6. Cut dough into two pieces; cover with a bowl and let rest for 20 minutes for easier shaping.
7. Grease a large baking sheet; sprinkle the sheet generously with cornmeal.
8. On a floured surface with a floured rolling pin, roll each piece into a 15-inch by 10-inch rectangle. From one of the longer sides, tightly roll the dough in a jelly-roll fashion; pinch the seam to seal.
9. Place loaves, seam side down, on the sheet and gently press the ends together. Brush with oil; loosely cover with plastic wrap; Refrigerate for 2 to 24 hours.
10. Preheat oven to 425°F. Remove loaves from refrigerator; uncover; let stand for 10 minutes. Cut diagonal slashes across the top of each loaf.
11. Bake for 20 minutes, then brush with egg white beaten with water (1 tbsp). Return to oven and bake an additional 5 minutes.
(42 min left), (H)elp, More?
